title: Change due date automation. Recipe : "“When date changes, adjust another date by the number of days in number”
excerpt:
Hello, I tried a recipe in one of my board. :point_down: 
“When date1 changes, adjust another date2 by the number of days in number”. 
What I don’t understand is that this automation was working fine 2 days ago. 
When I …

posts.author: ThiernoDiallo (Thierno)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello, I tried a recipe in one of my board.
“When date1 changes, adjust another date2 by the number of days in number”.

What I don’t understand is that this automation was working fine 2 days ago.
When I used to change the “date1” , the “date2” adjusted automatically.
Only weekdays were considered, just like I wanted. But now the weekend days

Any idea why this has changed now?

posts.author: anon29275264 (anon29275264)
posts.cookedHtml: Are you sure this is a monday.com provided recipe/action? I ask because I’ve never seen this built in - only from third party apps.

posts.author: mark.anley (Green Llamas Consulting)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @anon29275264 - this is indeed a standard monday automation. They have moved it around and it is now under the dependencies section:

@ThiernoDiallo - Did you change your date picker settings to now allow weekends? By default this automation will work for any date.

Thanks!
Mark

Green Llamas

monday.com + Green Llamas

Green Llamas is here to help you make all your People and Projects Green. Let us get your herds under control using the monday.com platform.

posts.author: anon29275264 (anon29275264)
posts.cookedHtml: Hmm. I think its coming back having seen it once or twice before, but it wasn’t available via normal methods. Like you could only get it via something in the dependencies column to create it?

posts.author: ThiernoDiallo (Thierno)
posts.cookedHtml:
In the administration menu I’ve selected “hide weekends” in the “Timeline weekends” section.
Which means that for all the users on the account are supposed to not be able to select a saturday or a sunday right?

posts.author: mark.anley (Green Llamas Consulting)
posts.cookedHtml: It’s only available as a template, however not connected to the dependency column in any way. It used to be stored under date automations.

posts.author: mark.anley (Green Llamas Consulting)
posts.cookedHtml: Thats correct. The setting is system wide and should not allow weekend selection.

posts.author: cmoscardelli (Carolyn Moscardelli)
posts.cookedHtml: Has anyone figured this out, I am having the same issue with this dependency, I have my settings set for NO WEEKENDS, but this automation is still allowing the adjusted date to land on a weekend.
